# Questions in Livestream 172

Live Stream #172: A Marathon of 14,000 Subs

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uSma5je3KA4?t=579" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">9:39</a> | If David Rohl rejects so much, why stay an Exodus historicist instead of becoming an Exodus mythicist? | He attributes that more to celebrity and attention than to consistent historical method. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uSma5je3KA4?t=778" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">12:58</a> | Was the golden calf in Exodus 32 based on an Egyptian god? | Yes. He connects it to an Egyptian sacred-cow myth in which a golden calf represents divine rebirth. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uSma5je3KA4?t=1019" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">16:59</a> | Why is Ahmose-Nefertari sometimes portrayed with black skin in art? | He says it is funerary or Osirian symbolism, not evidence that she was Nubian. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uSma5je3KA4?t=1462" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">24:22</a> | Would you consider doing a video series aimed at kids? | He says COPPA rules make child-targeted content restrictive even though the channel still aims to stay family-friendly. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uSma5je3KA4?t=1733" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">28:53</a> | How do I explain the Trinity to a five-year-old? | He says to keep it simple: the Father, Son, and Holy Spirit are each God, and the three are one God, with analogies treated cautiously. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uSma5je3KA4?t=1896" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">31:36</a> | How should I talk about Satan with a five-year-old who has trauma? | He recommends simple, protective language: God loves and protects, Satan is a bad angel who wants harm, and healthy boundaries matter. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uSma5je3KA4?t=2248" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">37:28</a> | How should I approach Genesis 1 with a five-year-old? | He suggests teaching Genesis 1 through sacred space and temple imagery, even using blocks to show God defining and filling space. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uSma5je3KA4?t=2618" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">43:38</a> | What is the best child-friendly Bible version? | He does not recommend a specific one, but says a good children's Bible should use simplified text, large print, and honest rather than sanitized pictures. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uSma5je3KA4?t=2787" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">46:27</a> | Is there enough time between a 1265 BC Exodus and Hazor's destruction for a 40-year wilderness period? | Yes. He dates Hazor's destruction closer to the 1220s and says there is enough room even if the 40 years are approximate. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uSma5je3KA4?t=2940" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">49:00</a> | Could you discuss the math-related Rhind or Ahmes Papyrus? | He identifies it as a Ramesside mathematical instructional papyrus and points to Robbins and Shute, plus Raymond Faulkner, for serious study. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uSma5je3KA4?t=5041" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:24:01</a> | Could you talk about Egyptian beekeeping and honey collection? | He describes Egyptian ceramic tube hives, honeycomb placement, mud closures, and harvest from the back of the hive. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uSma5je3KA4?t=5300" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:28:20</a> | Did Kathleen Kenyon discover evidence of a conflagration in Jericho's Late Bronze 2 strata? | He says it appears she did, even if that evidence was not clearly foregrounded in her reports. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uSma5je3KA4?t=5586" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:33:06</a> | Why are you a Sith? | He says the label came from Associates for Biblical Research calling him Palpatine, so he treats it as their nickname rather than his preference. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uSma5je3KA4?t=6427" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:47:07</a> | What does the Spirit do in baptism? | He says the Spirit is tied to newness of life and sanctification, while admitting denominational readings vary sharply. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uSma5je3KA4?t=7396" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:03:16</a> | What is the difference between redaction and retrojection? | He says retrojection uses present terminology for past things, while redaction changes an earlier author's content. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uSma5je3KA4?t=7631" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:07:11</a> | What does *pornoi* mean in 1 Corinthians 6:9? | He says it is a broad term for sexual activity outside marriage, with adultery as one kind but not the whole range. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uSma5je3KA4?t=7865" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:11:05</a> | Did schools of the prophets contribute to false prophets? | Possibly. He compares them to seminaries: institutions can train true servants, but some graduates may become false teachers. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uSma5je3KA4?t=7964" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:12:44</a> | What are the basic issues with Socratic, Platonic, and Aristotelian philosophy? | He says the question is too broad to answer well, though he notes that Plato, Xenophon, and Aristotle raise different issues for Christian use. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uSma5je3KA4?t=8125" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:15:25</a> | Is there a book, workbook, or course you recommend for new-believer discipleship classes? | He says he cannot recommend one and has not found many good options. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uSma5je3KA4?t=9813" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:43:33</a> | Is Egypt still safe to visit right now? | He says yes, while treating it as a tour-status question rather than a broad travel advisory. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uSma5je3KA4?t=13082" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:38:02</a> | Do all religions point to Christ? | No. He says some things may foreshadow Christ, but many religions point elsewhere. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uSma5je3KA4?t=13220" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:40:20</a> | Can you develop the idea that the Israelites were enslaved by Ahmose I? | He points to the biography of Ahmose son of Abana and the conquest of Avaris as the historical setting. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uSma5je3KA4?t=13576" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:46:16</a> | What are your thoughts on free will? | He says free will exists because he thinks the human mind and soul are not deterministic, and he connects that to middle knowledge. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uSma5je3KA4?t=13676" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:47:56</a> | Did studying the Exodus build your faith, or were you a Christian before? | He was already a Christian, but says studying Scripture and the Exodus did build his faith because he treated study as worship. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uSma5je3KA4?t=13875" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:51:15</a> | Is Moses' name related to Ramesses and Thutmose? | He says there are two main views; he favors an Egyptian *m-sh* connection, while acknowledging the *mose* view is also possible. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uSma5je3KA4?t=14674" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:04:34</a> | Does Mark have a low christology? | No. He says Mark still presents a high christology, including Jesus' divine authority. |
